data.description:
## Context / Problem

`CodeReviewBatchRunner.run` iterates only the static `req.criteria` list and stamps each finding
with `reviewer_id = prompt_id` and **no** logical criterion id
(`src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py:49,79`). The code-review gate YAML injects only the 13
built-in overlays (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml:77-94,109-126`), so an activated
project criterion never enters Round A/B and, even if it did, its findings could not be attributed
to `project.<name>`. Plan-review already solves the identical problem by fanning project criteria
in dynamically at runtime (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py:206-225`,
consuming `registry.effective_criteria`). This is the second of five seams (root cause on
superseded bug 8a4b).

## Approach

Mirror plan-review's runtime fan-in for the code-review arm:

- At the code-review batch assembly point in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` (where
  `CodeReviewBatchRunner` is constructed around `gate_dispatch.py:617-619` and `repo_root` is
  already available via `request.repo_root`), compute `registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)`
  (which exists at `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py:179-184`), select the `project.`-prefixed
  ids, resolve each to its physical prompt via
  `criterion_prompt_id(cid, gate_key="code_review") [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]`, and pass those
  project entries into the runner alongside the built-in criteria.
- In `batch_runner.py`, when emitting findings for a project criterion, stamp the **logical**
  `criteria = [cid]` in addition to the existing `reviewer_id` so Pass 3 and attribution can key
  on `project.<name>`.
- If an activated project criterion's prompt file is missing/malformed, raise a **located**
  `LLMError` (naming the criterion + expected prompt path) instead of silently skipping.

**Rejected alternative.** Adding static project slots to `code-review.yaml` — impossible: project
ids are per-repo and dynamic, which is exactly why plan-review fans them in at runtime rather than
via YAML. Reusing the runtime-fan-in pattern keeps the two gates symmetric.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` — assemble effective project criteria, thread into the
  runner.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py` — accept + iterate project criteria; stamp logical
  `criteria`; located error on missing prompt.
- `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` — extend the shared file; add the synthetic-repo
  fixture (writes `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` activating `project.foo` + a
  `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-foo.md` rubric) reused by later children.

## Testing

- Happy path (fan_in): the synthetic repo runs the `project.foo` finder exactly once in Round A and
  every emitted finding carries logical id `project.foo`.
- Parity: an overlay-absent repo produces a batch identical to today (no project entries; built-ins
  unchanged).
- Edge (config error): a `project.foo` activated with a missing prompt file raises a located
  `LLMError` naming the criterion and path.

Tier: unit with an on-disk synthetic-repo fixture (tmp_path); the finder LLM call is stubbed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The activated project criterion executes once per review and each finding carries logical
      `project.<name>`; verified by
      `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k fan_in`.
- [ ] An overlay-absent repo yields an unchanged built-in batch (parity); verified by
      `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k parity`.
- [ ] A project criterion with a missing prompt raises a located `LLMError`; verified by
      `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k missing`.
data.title: Fan project criteria into Round A/B and attribute logical ids
